steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence is: "Henry's careful construction ______ apparent." This is a subject-verb sentence with a linking verb. The subject is "Henry's careful construction" — the head noun is "construction," which is singular. The blank must be a verb that agrees with a singular third-person subject in present tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have become': "Have" is the plural present perfect auxiliary. Pairing it with the singular subject "construction" violates subject-verb agreement — it would require a plural noun like "constructions." ✗
  • Choice B — 'are becoming': "Are" is the plural present progressive auxiliary. Same error — the singular subject "construction" demands "is becoming," not "are becoming." ✗
  • Choice C — 'becomes': Third-person singular simple present. The -s ending signals agreement with a singular subject, matching "construction" perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'become': The base form without -s is used with plural subjects ("they become") or after modal verbs. Standing alone with a singular noun subject, it fails subject-verb agreement. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
